summary_one_line: An old-spec Indic syllable with a Halant (H) glyph positioned after the base consonant, which cmplog's I2S substitution actively disrupts.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte sequence decoding to an old-spec Indic syllable (indic_plan->is_old_spec true) where, after the base consonant, position i carries indic_category() == I_Cat(H) (a Halant), so the true side of line 678 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 with avg hitcount 34 on the branch; its winning seeds reach the true side via pure havoc/token byte mutation that happens to assemble the H-after-base glyph layout (e.g. seed bd95f0d9... carries an 'antd'/'do-hant-mo' style structural payload built by Crossover/Delete/Neg/Insert/BitFlip ops).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 and the branch hitcount is 0; its I2SRandReplace mutator overwrites byte runs to match intercepted CMP operands, displacing the carefully-positioned Halant category bytes so the post-base scan at line 677-678 never sees an H, leaving the true side unhit (DIVERGENT BRANCHES line 678 L: T=0 F=17).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Pair winner is naive (no I2S); the differentiating technique I2S is what the LOSER has. Plain havoc/token byte mutation in naive assembles the structural glyph sequence, while cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutions actively perturb the same bytes toward CMP operands, so the winner is enabled by havoc-only mutation, not by any comparison-solving.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (old-spec Indic syllable with a Halant glyph after the base consonant taking the true side of line 678), reachable when the value-profile gradient feedback rewards inputs whose category/codepoint bytes approach the H-after-base layout.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount 2); its CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial credit for byte sequences that move toward matching the comparison at line 678, retaining intermediate inputs that the loser discards.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) resolves 1/10 and blocks 9/10 with branch hitcount 0; lacking the CMP_MAP gradient it gets only all-or-nothing edge feedback and its I2S substitutions disrupt the H bytes, so it never retains the near-miss inputs needed to reach the true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: vpc beats cmplog and the differentiating technique is value_profile, so the enabling mechanism is the CMP_MAP gradient: Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward partial progress toward the H-category comparison at line 678, letting vpc climb to the true side where plain edge feedback (cmplog) stalls.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a Halant-category comparison inside old-spec Indic consonant reordering.
The blocked side is the true branch, and the enclosing guard requires old-spec mode scanning glyphs after the base consonant.
naive is the winner of pair 1 over cmplog with the I2S delta.
naive resolves 10/10 with a high branch hitcount while cmplog resolves 1/10 with zero hits.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=34  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: A naive winner seed carries a structural glyph payload assembled by pure havoc/token mutation ops.
cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes CMP operands into matching byte runs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line 678, the loser cmplog never takes the true side (T=0) while a resolving role takes it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 678  T=15 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (info[i].indic_category() == I_Cat(H))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog is the winner of pair 2 over cmplog with the value_profile delta.
vpc resolves 9/10 versus cmplog 1/10 at this branch.
value_profile_cmplog instrumentation includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets feeding the feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (pair 2, vpc>cmplog) and havoc-only (pair 1, naive>cmplog); neither winner relies on I2SRandReplace (cmplog is the LOSER in both pairs), so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. No dash-row I2S verification was needed because the I2S-bearing fuzzer is the blocked loser, not the winner.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on an exact CMP literal (where I2SRandReplace should help) showing cmplog resolving as well as or better than naive/vpc at this branch, which would contradict cmplog being the loser and indicate the divergence is comparison-solving rather than structural-disruption / gradient-retention.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2SRandReplace actively displaces the Halant bytes (rather than merely failing to help) is inferential from mechanism description plus the zero branch hitcount, not directly observed in a lineage trace.
